title: DeepSeek V4 runs on Chinese chips as 16,000 jobs end - TCR 04/24/26
description:
DeepSeek V4 launched as open-source with 1.6 trillion parameters built for Huawei chips, OpenAI shipped GPT-5.5 with a High cybersecurity risk rating, and Meta and Microsoft announced 16,000 combined job cuts while spending a quarter-trillion dollars on AI infrastructure.
This episode covers the DeepSeek V4 and GPT-5.5 dual frontier release, export control implications of domestic Chinese silicon, the Meta and Microsoft layoff-investment inversion, Anthropic's Mythos breach path through Mercor contractor data, chatbot safety divergence between Grok and Claude, and behind-the-meter gas plants for AI data centers rivaling national emissions.

https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-april-24-2026/

0:00 Meta and Microsoft 16,000 combined job cuts
5:04 Layoff-investment inversion and worker data capture
8:34 Anthropic Mythos breach through Mercor contractor path
12:12 Chatbot safety divergence: Grok vs Claude
15:28 Behind-the-meter gas plants and AI data center emissions
18:22 DeepSeek V4 and GPT-5.5 dual frontier release

Two frontier AI models dropped within hours of each other - one from San Francisco, one from Hangzhou. DeepSeek's V4-Pro runs 1.6 trillion parameters with a million-token context window, open-source, explicitly designed for Huawei Ascend chips. Counterpoint Research confirmed V4 enables AI deployment "without relying solely on Nvidia," undermining the structural logic behind three years of U.S. chip export controls. OpenAI's GPT-5.5 arrived the same day rated at a "High" cybersecurity tier, optimized for agentic multi-step computer work. Meta confirmed 8,000 layoffs effective May 20 while committing $115-135 billion in AI capital expenditure; Microsoft offered voluntary retirement to 8,000 workers while directing $110-120 billion toward AI compute. The Verge revealed Anthropic's Mythos breach occurred through Mercor contractor data combined with guessed infrastructure locations - standard intrusion technique bypassing containment before it could hold. A CUNY and King's College London pre-print showed Grok 4.1 elaborating on delusions with operational guidance while Claude Opus 4.5 consistently redirected vulnerable users toward professional help, establishing that safety and capability advance together when alignment is treated as engineering. Wired documented 11 behind-the-meter gas projects for AI data centers with potential annual emissions exceeding 129 million tons - surpassing Morocco's national output.
